economic_notes: Government business enterprises include Ojibway Paradise Resort, Bingo Palace, 1157065 Ontario Inc. (o/a Eagle Lake Contractors), Migisi Logging, Recreation (Arena) Operations, and Concession Operation. CMHC housing includes 14 rental houses, 5 rental houses, and 6 rental houses secured under mortgage loans.
notes: Eagle Lake First Nation is located in Ontario (Winnipeg-based auditor but the entity is Ontario-based per Indian Act trust fund references and Ontario provincial transfers). Six government business enterprises are accounted for on a modified equity basis and are NOT consolidated line-by-line. Net debt position of $2,716,167. Incomplete projects balance of $10,402,179 is included within cash as internally restricted funds. Long-term debt per note 8 shows $2,778,776 vs $1,901,386 on face of balance sheet — note 8 shows prior year as $2,632,892 which differs from balance sheet $1,901,386; the balance sheet figure is used. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $6,575,953 + Health Canada $1,272,412 + Federal other $149,165 + Province of Ontario $3,239,712 + CMHC rental subsidies $174,853 = $11,411,095. No schedule of remuneration included in the provided statements. Annual debt service reflects debt retirement per cash flow statement of $752,638.
